definition_en: This section covers the principles of resolution and the mechanics of how skills take effect. The rules here are the basis for resolving all types of conflicting situations.

rules.en: When an example refers to "a character" without qualification, it means the character has no General Card skills or equipment skills â€” treated as a blank.
This section covers: Resolution Principles (Â§4.1), Elements of Skills (Â§4.2), Topic 3: Locked Skills and Priority (Â§4.3), Topic 4: Classifying Skills and Cards (Â§4.4).

search_text_en: rules guide overview resolution principles skill elements

definition_en: The fundamental principles for resolving conflicting effects and determining resolution order. Two categories: Conflict Principles (how to handle skill-vs-rule or skill-vs-skill conflicts) and Order Principles (how to sequence actions for multiple or single characters).

rules.en: Conflict Principle â‘ : When a skill's description conflicts with a general rule or terminology standard, the priority is: Skill description > Basic/Tool card description > General rules or terminology standards.

segments.en: Priority: Skill description > Basic/Tool card description > General rules or terminology standards.

examples.en: Zhang Fei's [Roar] conflicts with the Basic card rule that [Kill] can only be used once per Action Phase. The skill description takes priority.

rules.en: Conflict Principle â‘¡: When two skills' effects or operations conflict, a negative effect (prohibition) takes priority over a positive effect (permission).

segments.en: A negative effect (prohibition) takes priority over a positive effect (permission).

examples.en: After winning with [Heaven's Justice], Tai Shi Ci's [Kill] has no distance restriction (positive). [Entrap the Heirâ‘¡] requires a distance check (negative). Since negative takes priority, no distance check is needed.

rules.en: Insertion Order Principle: Any inserted event is resolved first â€” the most recently triggered event resolves first. If skill A produces timing b while executing its costs or effects, skill B (with timing b) may insert. After B's resolution completes, A continues.

segments.en: The most recently triggered event resolves first. If skill A produces timing b while executing its costs or effects, skill B (with timing b) may insert and resolve first, then A continues.

examples.en: Xiao Qiao activates [Celestial Fragrance] to transfer damage to Wei Yan. Wei Yan receives damage and [Bloodthirst] inserts, recovering HP. Since Wei Yan's HP Lost is now 0, the [Celestial Fragrance] draw effect does not execute.

rules.en: Multi-Character Order Principle a: When one card, skill, or event resolves against multiple targets, responses proceed counter-clockwise starting from the current turn's character. Unless otherwise specified, each character may respond only once.

segments.en: When resolving against multiple targets, proceed counter-clockwise from the current turn's character; each character may respond only once unless otherwise specified.

examples.en: Zhang Liao activates [Sudden Raid] against two targets. He gains one hand card from each, proceeding counter-clockwise from himself.

rules.en: Multi-Character Order Principle b: When multiple characters share the same timing and all have skills/effects at that timing, they activate in counter-clockwise order starting from the current turn's character.

segments.en: When multiple characters share the same timing and all have skills at that timing, they activate in counter-clockwise order from the current turn's character.

examples.en: Lady Wu uses [Sweet Dew] on Ling Tong and Sun Shangxiang, who simultaneously lose all equipment. [Whirlwindâ‘ ], [Fierce Lady], and [Silver Lion Helmetâ‘¡] all share this timing. Starting from Lady Wu, they decide in counter-clockwise order.

rules.en: Multi-Character Order Principle c: When a skill can activate once per target when designating multiple targets, activation decisions are made counter-clockwise from the current turn's character, once for each target.

segments.en: Skills that activate once per target: decisions are made counter-clockwise from the current turn's character, once for each target.

examples.en: Ma Chao uses [Kill] on three targets (via [Sky Piercer]). After designating targets, starting from the current turn's character counter-clockwise, he decides whether to activate [Iron Cavalry] for each target in order.

rules.en: Multi-Character Order Principle d: When skill A triggers "whenever a character is at this timing," A resolves counter-clockwise from the current turn's character, once per applicable character. Condition checks and cost execution happen immediately before each decision.

segments.en: For skills that trigger "whenever a character is at this timing," proceed counter-clockwise from the current turn's character, once per applicable character. Condition checks and cost execution happen immediately before each decision.

rules.en: Order when no turn is active: When a character's turn ends due to death mid-resolution â€” from resolution start to "At Death": counter-clockwise from that character; from after "At Death" to the next turn: counter-clockwise from their Right Neighbor. After a turn ends: if alive, from that character; if dead, from their Right Neighbor. Before a turn starts: counter-clockwise from that character.

segments.en: Order when no turn is active:

segments.en: From resolution start to "At Death" â†’ counter-clockwise from that character; after "At Death" to next turn â†’ from their Right Neighbor. After turn ends: alive â†’ from that character; dead â†’ from Right Neighbor. Before turn starts â†’ counter-clockwise from that character.

rules.en: Single-Character Order Principle a: When a character can activate general skills, equipment skills, and card effects at the same timing, the order is: General skill first â†’ Equipment skill second â†’ Card use/effect last.

segments.en: When general skills, equipment skills, and card effects all share a timing: General skill â†’ Equipment skill â†’ Card effect.

examples.en: Lord Cao Cao (equipped with [Eight Trigrams Formation]) needs to use [Dodge]: first activate [Imperial Guard]; if no Wei character plays [Dodge], then activate [Eight Trigrams Formation]; if the result is black, finally use a hand card [Dodge].

rules.en: Single-Character Order Principle b: When a character can activate multiple general skills (or the same skill multiple times) at the same timing, they choose the order. Condition checks, target legality checks, and cost execution all happen immediately before each individual activation decision.

segments.en: When multiple general skills share a timing, the character chooses the order. Condition checks, target legality, and cost execution all happen immediately before each individual activation decision.

examples.en: God Sima Yi (who has triggered [Seal of Command] and has no Forbearance tokens) takes 1 damage. He can choose to first trigger [Forbearanceâ‘ ] to gain a token, then immediately spend it to activate [Exile].

rules.en: When multiple skills can activate at the same timing, the first skill activated may cause that timing to end, preventing subsequent skills from activating.

examples.en: When Ma Dai (equipped with [Frost Blade]) uses [Kill] and deals damage, [Covert Strike] can activate before [Frost Blade]. If [Covert Strike] activates and prevents the damage, the game proceeds directly to "At Damage Resolution End" â€” [Frost Blade] no longer has a timing to activate.

rules.en: At any given timing, unless a skill is count-based (e.g. activates once per target or once per damage point), a character has exactly one opportunity to decide whether to activate it â€” unless otherwise specified.

definition_en: Skills are divided into state skills (no activation timing; must produce their effect if they can) and non-state skills (have an activation timing). Non-state skills consist of six elements: activating character, condition, timing, cost, target, and effect.

rules.en: Activation flow: At the activation timing, if the condition is met, declare the skill name while simultaneously selecting legal targets and paying all costs. If more than one cost cannot be paid, the skill cannot be activated and no costs may be paid. Once all costs are paid, the effects must be executed.

segments.en: At the activation timing, if the condition is met, declare the skill name while simultaneously selecting legal targets and paying all costs. If more than one cost cannot be paid, the skill cannot be activated and no costs may be paid. Once all costs are paid, the effects must be executed.

rules.en: Cost categories: â‘  Moving cards (discard, Point Duel, give, etc.); â‘¡ Place cards from General Card into Discard Pile; â‘¢ Remove tokens; â‘£ Flip over; â‘¤ Lose HP (any amount, as long as current HP Value > 0); â‘¥ Skip a phase; â‘¦ Draw fewer cards; â‘§ Forfeit drawing; â‘¨ Prevent damage; â‘© Reveal hand cards; â‘ª Other.

segments.en: Moving cards (discard, Point Duel, give, etc.);
Place cards from General Card into Discard Pile;
Lose HP (any amount, as long as current HP Value > 0);

rules.en: A character whose HP Value is 0 cannot pay a Lose HP cost to activate a skill.
If an event's effect happens to match a skill's cost, it does not count as the character having paid that cost.

examples.en: Xiahou Yuan's [Acedia] judgment fails and he must skip his Action Phase. He cannot activate [Lightning Speedâ‘¡] because skipping the Action Phase is that skill's cost â€” the external effect does not count as paying the cost.

rules.en: A non-state skill with no cost can still be activated as long as at least one of its effects can execute. If none of its effects can execute, it cannot be activated.

examples.en: When no other character has any cards, Tian Feng cannot activate [Remonstrate to Death]. An unwounded Wei Yan does not trigger [Bloodthirst].

rules.en: A character can only activate a skill if both themselves and the target are alive/present at the activation timing.

examples.en: After the [Raining Arrows] user dies, Sima Yi receives damage but cannot activate [Retribution] because the source no longer exists.

rules.en: If a skill's first effect is to use a card, the legal required targets for that card must be selected in advance as the skill's targets, and a second legality check is conducted when the effect actually executes.

examples.en: The target selected for Xiahou Yuan's [Lightning Speed] must be a legal target for his [Kill].

rules.en: If a character activates a skill and gains a new skill whose activation timing is the same timing, they are still within that timing and may immediately activate the new skill.

examples.en: Jiang Wei triggers [Inherit the Will] at Starting Phase Start and gains [Read the Stars] â€” he may immediately activate it. Zuo Ci activates [Shapeshifterâ‘¡] after his turn ends and gains [Supreme Advance] â€” he may immediately activate it.

search_text_en: skill elements activation condition timing cost target effect state non-state

definition_en: "Locked Skill" is just a label indicating that the skill is mandatory â€” it has absolutely no relationship with priority and grants no special privileges.

rules.en: "Locked Skill" is just a label, not a type. Whether a skill is mandatory depends on whether the skill text contains "may" (å¯ä»¥), not on whether it says "Locked Skill." No "may" = mandatory. Locked Skills do not activate first, do not activate earlier, and do not override other skills â€” their only special property is being mandatory.

segments.en: "Locked Skill" is just a label. Whether mandatory: no "may" in skill text = mandatory. Does not activate first, does not activate earlier, does not override other skills. The only special property is being mandatory.

rules.en: State Locked Skills do not need to be triggered and have no activation timing element. They must produce their effect whenever they can.

examples.en: Once Zhuge Liang (Std) with hand cards is designated as a target of [Kill] or [Duel], if he loses all hand cards during the resolution, [Empty Fort] no longer applies â€” the [Kill] or [Duel] still resolves normally.

rules.en: Non-state Locked Skills must activate at their timing if they can, but do not activate with higher priority, earlier, or merely because their condition is met before the timing arrives.

examples.en: Zhang Chunhua uses [Eight Trigrams Formation] from her hand, simultaneously losing a hand card and [Silver Lion Helmet] from her Equipment Zone. General skill [Mourn the Fallen] activates before equipment skill [Silver Lion Helmetâ‘¡], even though the latter is a Locked Skill.

rules.en: Beyond a skill's own listed conditions, the effects of other already-active skills (whether Locked or not) can also restrict that skill from triggering.

examples.en: The effects of [Qing Gang Sword] and [Charge the Phalanx] both restrict [Silver Lion Helmetâ‘ ] from triggering.

rules.en: The effects of Locked Skills do not always have to execute â€” they are also subject to the rules in Topic 4 regarding effect execution.

search_text_en: locked skill priority state mandatory forced

definition_en: Once an event begins resolving, it must follow the prescribed flow: execute all executable operations, skip those that cannot. Non-state skill effects are divided into: state effects (no execution timing; must produce their effect if they can) and non-state effects (have an execution timing), further divided into delayed and non-delayed effects.

rules.en: When a character must execute an effect on cards not visible to all, and the number of cards they can legally operate on is both less than the required number and less than the total available, they must reveal those cards and operate on all legally operable ones.

examples.en: Yang Xiu targets a character with [Chicken Rib] selecting equipment cards. That character (HP 2) has two [Dodge]s and three equipment cards in hand. During Discard Phase, they must reveal all hand cards and discard the two [Dodge]s.

rules.en: When a living character loses a general skill, cards on/beside their General Card and all tokens are unaffected. Whether a state effect persists after the skill is lost depends on whether it is tied to the character who lost it: if tied (e.g. [Total Annihilation] is tied to Jia Xu), it disappears; if not tied (e.g. [Chicken Rib]'s restriction applies to the designated character, not Yang Xiu), it persists.

segments.en: State effects after losing a skill:

segments.cn: è‹¥æ•ˆæžœä¸Žå¤±åŽ»æŠ€èƒ½çš„è§’è‰²æœ‰å…³ â†’ æ•ˆæžœæ¶ˆå¤±ï¼›è‹¥æ•ˆæžœä¸Žè¯¥è§’è‰²æ— å…³ â†’ æ•ˆæžœä¸æ¶ˆå¤±ã€‚
segments.en: If the effect is tied to the character who lost the skill â†’ effect disappears; if not tied â†’ effect persists.

examples.en: Yang Xiu activates [Chicken Rib] then kills Cai Wenji; [Heartbreak] triggers and Yang Xiu loses all skills â€” but [Chicken Rib]'s effect persists. Jia Xu kills Cai Wenji in his own turn, loses all skills â€” [Total Annihilation]'s effect ends.

rules.en: Whether a non-state delayed effect continues after a character loses the skill or dies depends on whether the effect is tied to that character: if it is (e.g. [Gale]/[Great Fog] are tied to God Zhuge Liang), the effect ends; if it is not (e.g. [Celestial Fragrance]'s draw effect is not tied to Xiao Qiao), the effect persists.

segments.cn: è‹¥æ•ˆæžœä¸Žè¯¥è§’è‰²æœ‰å…³ â†’ æ•ˆæžœæ¶ˆå¤±ï¼›è‹¥æ•ˆæžœä¸Žè¯¥è§’è‰²æ— å…³ â†’ æ•ˆæžœä¸æ¶ˆå¤±ã€‚
segments.en: If the effect is tied to that character â†’ effect disappears; if not tied â†’ effect persists.

examples.en: God Zhuge Liang activates [Gale]/[Great Fog] then dies before his next turn â€” the effects disappear. Xiao Qiao activates [Celestial Fragrance] to transfer damage then dies â€” the draw effect still executes.

rules.en: Once a non-state non-delayed effect begins executing, all executable effects must be executed in sequence even if the character loses the skill or dies.

segments.en: Once a non-delayed effect begins executing:
even if the character loses the skill or dies, all executable effects must execute in sequence.

examples.en: Zhang Jue uses [Frost Blade] to discard Deng Ai's first card, then plays [Frost Blade] out via [Ghost Path] â€” losing the skill â€” but since activation succeeded, he must still discard Deng Ai's second card. Lu Bu (with [Sky Piercer]) uses [Kill] on Cai Wenji and A; [Peerlessâ‘ ] triggers; Cai Wenji's [Heartbreak] causes Lu Bu to lose all skills, but [Peerlessâ‘ ] still executes when resolving against A.
